subject: chore(scheduling): apply migration to remote + full smoke test + docs sync
body:
Applied 20260421024318_add_scheduled_messages to kmqcbpeidmmintltcoqm via
the Management API's /database/query endpoint (direct Postgres pooler
ports are blocked from the web sandbox, so supabase db push can't run
here). Recorded the row in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations.

Verified remote state: table + 3 named indexes + pkey, 2 triggers,
5 functions, 3 RLS policies (no DELETE by design), both cron jobs
registered (dispatcher every minute + cleanup 04:17 UTC), realtime
publication membership.

Ran 20 end-to-end smoke cases, all passing: every CHECK constraint,
the UPDATE trigger for lead-time bounds, updated_at bumping, dispatcher
happy path + all failure branches (too_stale, chat_ended, rate_limited,
reply-retracted-snippet-nulling), full RLS matrix for both users,
check_rate_limit('schedule', ...) correctness, and cron-only function
grants. All test rows cleaned up, no residue.

CLAUDE.md AI Notes corrected to match reality: actual env var names
(UTIL_SUPABASE_* + SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN_CLAUDE_WEB, not the literal
names the CLI expects), current sandbox connectivity (Management API
reachable, direct pooler blocked), and the shared-DB situation. New
AI_MISTAKES entry captures the env-var-assumption lesson. SESSION_NOTES
rewritten as a fresh snapshot; HISTORY updated with the detailed
smoke-test outcome; TODO records the deferred legacy-table cleanup
that still needs user sign-off because the DB is shared.

Tags: scheduling, database, migration, smoke-test, docs
